pages.title: About Us - Haire's Gulf Shrimp
pages.metaDescription: Haire’s Gulf Shrimp is a proud Black-owned, women-led business that has become a beloved part of the Greater Grand Crossing neighborhood on Chicago’s South Side. Originally launched by Finnie Haire in the early 1980s from a train caboose, our seafood restaurant is renowned for serving the best fried shrimp in Chicago, featuring our signature Louisiana-style shrimp.

headings.text: The culinary legacy of our famous seafood restaurant is deeply rooted in family.

sections.heading: Haire's Gulf Shrimp
body: Menus & Locations Vincennes Ave South Loop
Menus & Locations Vincennes Ave South Loop

sections.heading: Haire's Famous Louisiana Shrimp
body: Haire’s Gulf Shrimp is a proud Black-owned, women-led business that has become a beloved part of the Greater Grand Crossing neighborhood on Chicago’s South Side. Originally launched by Finnie Haire in the early 1980s from a train caboose, our seafood restaurant is renowned for serving the best fried shrimp in Chicago, featuring our signature Louisiana-style shrimp.

Haire’s mother, affectionately known as “Big Mama,” passed down her cherished shrimp recipe, creating a tradition that resonates with our community. Michael Haire Baldwin, Finnie Haire’s nephew, fondly recalls the afternoon in the ‘70s when his grandmother invited them into her kitchen to learn the art of making fried shrimp—a moment that has become a treasured family story.

Haire’s Gulf Shrimp is committed to excellence in corporate catering and seafood catering services in Chicago. We’re known for our shrimp dinners with fries, shrimp bomb bags, and our food truck that brings the taste of our historic restaurant to events across the city. Join us to experience why we’re considered the best shrimp spot on Chicago’s South Side!

sections.heading: Finnie Haire
body: Finnie Haire originally launched the locally famous shrimp shack in the early 1980s from a train caboose. The restaurant is currently located in the Greater Grand Crossing neighborhood on 75th and Vincennes.

Haire’s mother, affectionately called “Big Mama” by family, passed the shrimp recipe down according to Michael Haire Baldwin, nephew of Finnie Haire. Baldwin vividly recalls the afternoon, back in the ‘70s, when his grandmother called him and Haire into her kitchen to teach them how to make fried shrimp. The experience has since become a treasured family story.
Haire’s Gulf Shrimp is a family business and a beloved part of the neighborhood.

sections.heading: In the news
body: Finnie Haire Launched A South Side Fried Shrimp Business And Created A Family Legacy: ‘No One Stood In His Way’
Chance the Rapper Talks About the First Time He Met Obama
Haire’s Gulf Shrimp serves up fresh, fried shrimp on the South Side

pages.text: HAIRE'S GULF SHRIMP MENUS & LOCATIONS CATERING ABOUT US ORDER ONLINE Wholesale information for retail buyers, distributors, and brokers. Haire's Gulf Shrimp ​Authentic Louisiana Taste Shrimp Batter and Breading Mix Craving crispy & flavorful Louisiana fried shrimp? The secret’s out! Unlock 20 years of shrimp-frying wisdom with Finnie Haire’s authentic batter mix. Exclusively used by Chicago’s legendary shrimp house, Haire’s Gulf Shrimp. This recipe finally lets you bring that restaurant-quality taste straight to your kitchen. Ditch the bland and boring and get ready for a taste of Louisiana tradition. Preparation 1. Mix 2. Batter 3. Fry 4. Enjoy Ingredients Wheat flour, sugar, hot sauce powder, dehydrates garlic and onion, salt, spices, paprika, tricalcium, phosphate, paprika extract, natural flavor. Kosher Certified. Product information Manufacturer Product Number: 5529 Purchase/Portion Pack Size: 16/10-oz (zip sealed, top heat sealed) UPC: 732388394432 Lead Time: 14 days Shipping information Case dimensions (LxWxH): 8.5″ x 7.75″ x 6.5″ Case Net Weight: 10 lbs Case Gross Weight: 11 lbs Storage Temperature: 45-75° F Shelf life: 2 years HI/TI: 30 Layer x 5 High Total Cases Per Pallet: 140 Wholesale orders For information about placing a wholesale order, please submit the form below.
